APEX-001.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended for use within NHS England via Cancer Drugs Fund. Managed access agreement in place with annual clinical review. Subcutaneous formulation approved for home administration pathway.
APEX-001.evidence_gap: Long-term OS data pending 5-year POLLUX/CASTOR follow-up

APEX-001.reimbursement_strategy: Joint clinical assessment concluded added therapeutic benefit versus bortezomib-based SoC. National P&R negotiations ongoing in DE, FR, IT. Risk-share arrangements expected in price-sensitive markets.
APEX-001.evidence_gap: Comparative OS data vs. KRd needed for DE AMNOG dossier

APEX-002.indication: RRMM (4L+ post ASCT and prior IMiD/PI/anti-CD38)
APEX-002.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended only within a CDF managed access scheme due to ICER threshold breach at list price. Patient Access Scheme (confidential discount) submitted. Mandatory patient-level outcome data collection required for continuation review in 24 months.
APEX-002.evidence_gap: OS maturity insufficient at data cut; PFS2 data requested by committee

APEX-002.reimbursement_strategy: ICER found ciltacabtagene autoleucel cost-effective only at a net price of ~$380,000–$410,000 (vs. WAC ~$465,000). Negotiated outcomes-based contracts with 3 major PBMs in discussion. Manufacturing throughput constraints limiting access independently.
APEX-002.evidence_gap: Real-world durability data post 24 months; CARTITUDE-4 longer follow-up

APEX-003.indication: NSCLC with EGFR Exon 20 insertion mutations (1L+)
APEX-003.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended as routine commissioning within NHS England. Companion diagnostic (OncomineDx) funded through NHSE molecular testing programme. CMAP registry enrolment required for continued access.
APEX-003.evidence_gap: OS data immature at approval; 36-month landmark analysis expected 2027

APEX-003.reimbursement_strategy: Joint assessment concluded 'added benefit — non-quantifiable' due to absence of head-to-head versus osimertinib in this setting. National negotiations in FR, ES expected to require registry-linked outcomes data. DE AMNOG review requesting ITC submission.
APEX-003.evidence_gap: No direct comparison vs. osimertinib; ITC quality disputed by GBA

APEX-004.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended as a treatment option within the IL-23 inhibitor class. NICE confirmed superiority versus ustekinumab and non-inferiority versus other IL-23s. List price accepted; no patient access scheme required.
Joint assessment confirmed added benefit in PsA with predominant skin involvement. Accepted across majority of EU5 markets. Spain requires AEMPS registry. Italy requires cost-sharing agreement.
APEX-004.evidence_gap: Radiographic progression data at 2-year mark not yet mature

APEX-005.reimbursement_strategy: Early assessment (Article 5(1)) initiated pre-approval. Preliminary opinion acknowledges unmet need but requests comparative evidence versus eculizumab and efgartigimod. Adaptive pathway designation under discussion with EMA. Risk-sharing model expected.
APEX-005.evidence_gap: No head-to-head vs. FcRn class competitors; OS/QoL endpoints needed

APEX-005.reimbursement_strategy: ICER pre-launch economic model review suggests cost-effectiveness threshold likely exceeded at anticipated WAC unless outcomes-based contract structured around MGFA-PIS responder rates. J&J Market Access is modelling 3 pricing scenarios for ICER submission.
APEX-005.evidence_gap: ICER requesting blinded 6-month remission rate from Phase 3 FJORD trial

APEX-006.indication: Treatment-Resistant Depression (TRD) — add-on to oral antidepressant
APEX-006.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended only within a managed access programme for patients who have failed ≥2 antidepressants. Administered in certified healthcare settings only. Cost-per-responder model with NHS England; outcomes collected via Optimise registry.
APEX-006.evidence_gap: Long-term relapse prevention data >52 weeks; real-world dissociation rates

APEX-006.indication: TRD and MDD with acute suicidal ideation (MDSI)
APEX-006.reimbursement_strategy: ICER 2024 review concluded esketamine is cost-effective in MDSI at current net price. TRD indication remains borderline; outcomes-based contracts with major payers tied to 6-month remission rates.
APEX-006.evidence_gap: Comparative durability vs. emerging oral TRD agents (zuranolone, Auvelity)

APEX-007.reimbursement_strategy: Recommended as a treatment option for adults with active RMS. Positioned within NHS treatment algorithm between high-efficacy oral agents and IV anti-CD20 therapies. No patient access scheme; SMC Scotland review pending Q3 2026.
APEX-007.evidence_gap: Long-term lymphocyte recovery data post-discontinuation; 5-year follow-up

APEX-007.reimbursement_strategy: Joint assessment confirmed moderate added benefit vs. teriflunomide and dimethyl fumarate. Accepted across DE, FR, IT at submitted price. ES requires post-marketing registry. Reimbursement in NL pending ZIN guidance expected Q4 2026.
APEX-007.evidence_gap: Comparative MRI activity data vs. ofatumumab at 2-year mark
